name: Book Of Watchers
shortDescription: The Book of Watchers is the first and arguably most influential section of 1 Enoch, detailing the fall of the Watchers (fallen angels) and their impact on humanity.
description: The Book of Watchers constitutes chapters 1-36 of 1 Enoch, a pseudepigraphal work attributed to Enoch, the great-grandfather of Noah. It narrates the story of the Watchers, a group of angels sent to Earth to watch over humanity. However, some of these Watchers, led by figures like Azazel and Semjaza, lust after human women and descend from their heavenly abode on Mount Hermon, swearing an oath to carry out their plan. They interbreed with humans, producing the Nephilim, giants of immense power and wickedness. These giants consume all the resources of mankind, and then turn to consuming mankind itself. The Watchers also teach humanity forbidden knowledge, such as the art of warfare, cosmetics, and metalworking, leading to further corruption and societal breakdown. God, observing the wickedness that has overtaken the Earth, sends the archangels to intervene. Raphael is tasked with binding Azazel and casting him into the desert, while Gabriel is sent to destroy the Nephilim. Michael is commanded to bind Semjaza and his fellow Watchers. Enoch, in a dream vision, ascends to heaven and intercedes on behalf of the Watchers, but his plea is rejected. The Book of Watchers establishes the foundation for the rest of 1 Enoch, influencing later Jewish apocalyptic literature, including the Dead Sea Scrolls, and impacting early Christian thought. The narrative addresses themes of divine justice, the origin of evil, and the consequences of transgression.

sources.title: Outside the Bible: Ancient Jewish Writings Related to Scripture
sources.author: Louis H. Feldman, James L. Kugel, Lawrence H. Schiffman
